summary.title: Scenario 3 - Microservices Healthcare Application
summary.description: A healthcare microservices patient management system with Patient Portal, API Gateway, Identity Service, Medical Records Service, Appointment Service, and Notification Service.

data.name: Public Internet
data.description: Untrusted external zone. All traffic from here is treated as hostile until authenticated.

data.name: DMZ (Perimeter Zone)
data.description: Perimeter zone containing the Patient Portal and API Gateway. Only components exposed to the public internet. Separates untrusted internet traffic from all internal services.

data.name: Internal Services Zone
data.description: Trusted internal network zone hosting all backend microservices. Not accessible from the public internet. All traffic must enter through the API Gateway.

data.name: Third-Party Providers Zone
data.description: External untrusted zone hosting third-party SMS and email delivery providers. All outbound communications must be encrypted.

data.name: Patient
data.description: End user accessing the healthcare portal from a browser over the public internet to view records and book appointments

data.name: Patient Portal
data.description: Web frontend exposed to the public internet. Patients log in, view medical records, and book appointments. Communicates with the API Gateway for all backend operations.

threats.title: Patient Portal Phishing and Credential Harvesting
threats.description: An attacker creates a convincing replica of the patient portal login page and tricks patients into submitting their credentials via phishing emails. Captured credentials are then used to impersonate legitimate patients and access their medical records.
threats.mitigation: Enforce multi-factor authentication for all patient accounts. Implement HSTS and certificate pinning. Monitor for login anomalies such as unusual geolocations or unrecognised devices. Align with OWASP Top 10 A07 Identification and Authentication Failures.

threats.title: Cross-Site Scripting on Patient Portal
threats.description: An attacker injects malicious JavaScript through unsanitised user input fields such as appointment notes. When other patients or staff view the affected pages the script executes in their browser, stealing session tokens or redirecting to malicious sites.
threats.mitigation: Implement a strict Content Security Policy header. Sanitise and HTML-encode all user-supplied output server-side. Set HttpOnly and Secure flags on all session cookies. Perform regular penetration testing of frontend input fields.

data.name: API Gateway
data.description: Single entry point for all inbound requests to internal microservices. Routes authenticated requests to appropriate backend services and enforces access control policies.

threats.title: API Gateway DDoS Rendering All Services Unavailable
threats.description: An attacker floods the API Gateway with a high volume of requests, exhausting its processing capacity. Since all internal services are routed exclusively through the gateway, a successful attack makes the entire system unavailable, preventing patients from accessing records or booking urgent appointments.
threats.mitigation: Deploy rate limiting and throttling per client IP and per authenticated user. Integrate a cloud-based DDoS mitigation service in front of the gateway. Implement auto-scaling and circuit breakers. Align with NIST 800-53 SC-5 Denial of Service Protection.

threats.title: Internal Endpoints Exposed via Gateway Misconfiguration
threats.description: A misconfigured routing rule or overly permissive gateway policy inadvertently exposes internal medical record endpoints to the public internet without authentication. An attacker enumerating API paths could retrieve protected health information without any valid credentials.
threats.mitigation: Apply a whitelist-only approach to API Gateway routing, denying all unlisted paths by default. Enforce JWT validation at the gateway before routing any request. Conduct regular gateway configuration audits and automated security scanning. Align with OWASP API Security Top 10 API3 Broken Object Property Level Authorisation.

data.name: Identity Service
data.description: Handles authentication and authorisation using OAuth 2.0. Issues signed JSON Web Tokens to authenticated users. All internal services verify tokens through this service.

threats.title: JWT Token Forgery via Weak Signing Key
threats.description: If the Identity Service uses a weak or default secret key to sign JWTs, an attacker can forge arbitrary tokens granting themselves any patient identity or administrative role. All internal services accepting these tokens without proper verification would process malicious requests as authenticated.
threats.mitigation: Use RS256 asymmetric signing with a minimum 2048-bit RSA key pair. Store private keys in an HSM or secrets manager. Rotate signing keys periodically. Implement short JWT expiry times. All services must validate token signature, issuer, audience, and expiry on every request. Align with NIST 800-53 IA-5.

threats.title: OAuth Scope Escalation via Identity Service
threats.description: An attacker manipulates the OAuth 2.0 authorisation request to the Identity Service, requesting broader scopes than entitled, for example requesting write access to medical records when only read access to their own appointments should be granted. An overly permissive Identity Service issues the inflated JWT, granting excess permissions across all internal services.
threats.mitigation: Enforce strict server-side scope validation, ignoring any client-requested scopes not explicitly pre-approved. Implement least privilege for all OAuth scopes. Log all token issuance events with requested and granted scopes for audit purposes.

data.name: Medical Records Service
data.description: Stores and retrieves patient medical records including diagnoses, prescriptions, and test results. Verifies JWTs with the Identity Service before processing any request.

threats.title: Unauthorised Record Access via Broken Object Level Authorisation
threats.description: The Medical Records Service does not enforce object-level authorisation checks. An authenticated patient can access other patients records by simply modifying the patient ID in the request. This allows mass exposure of protected health information for any patient in the system.
threats.mitigation: Implement mandatory object-level authorisation for every record operation, verifying the requesting user identity in the JWT matches the patient ID of the requested record. Apply OWASP API Security Top 10 API1 Broken Object Level Authorisation controls. Conduct regular authorisation penetration testing.

threats.title: Medical Record Tampering via Compromised Service Account
threats.description: An attacker compromising an internal service account can send authenticated requests to modify or delete patient records, alter diagnoses, or remove prescriptions. Without write integrity controls the tampered data is indistinguishable from legitimate records, posing a direct patient safety risk.
threats.mitigation: Enforce role-based access control distinguishing read-only patient access from write access reserved for clinical staff. Implement cryptographic signing of medical record entries. Enable immutable audit logging for all create, update, and delete operations. Follow NIST 800-53 SI-7 Software and Information Integrity.

threats.title: Absence of Medical Record Access Audit Logs
threats.description: Without comprehensive immutable audit logging a malicious insider who accesses or modifies patient records can deny having done so. This also makes it impossible to satisfy HIPAA audit control requirements, investigate data breaches, or detect patterns of unauthorised access.
threats.mitigation: Implement append-only audit logging for all access and modification events, capturing user identity, timestamp, action, record ID, and source IP. Store logs in a separate tamper-evident log store. Retain logs as required by healthcare regulations. Align with ISO 27001 A.12.4 Logging and Monitoring.

data.name: Appointment Service
data.description: Manages patient appointment scheduling and cancellations. Reads patient data from the Medical Records Service and triggers reminders via the Notification Service.

threats.title: Appointment Changes without Non-Repudiation
threats.description: The Appointment Service does not maintain a verifiable record of who booked, modified, or cancelled an appointment and when. A rogue staff member could cancel critical appointments and deny responsibility with no audit evidence available, posing both a patient safety risk and a regulatory compliance failure.
threats.mitigation: Log all appointment events with the authenticated user identity, timestamp, and change details in an immutable audit trail. Implement digital signatures on appointment records for cryptographic non-repudiation. Align with HIPAA audit control requirements under 45 CFR 164.312.

data.name: Notification Service
data.description: Sends appointment reminders to patients via SMS and email using third-party provider APIs. Receives notification requests from the Appointment Service.

threats.title: Patient Health Information Leakage in Notification Payloads
threats.description: The Notification Service includes excessive patient health information such as diagnosis details in SMS or email payloads sent to third-party providers. Once this data crosses the trust boundary to an external provider the organisation loses control over it, risking PHI exposure in violation of HIPAA if the provider is breached.
threats.mitigation: Enforce data minimisation in all outbound notifications, including only appointment date and time. Never include diagnosis or medication details. Encrypt all payloads in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher. Review third-party provider data agreements for HIPAA compliance. Apply NIST 800-53 SC-8 Transmission Confidentiality.

threats.title: Notification Service Abuse for Message Flooding
threats.description: An attacker exploiting a vulnerability in the Appointment Service can trigger the Notification Service to dispatch large volumes of SMS or email messages, exhausting third-party provider quotas and API rate limits. This disables legitimate appointment reminders for all patients and may incur significant financial cost.
threats.mitigation: Implement strict per-user and per-appointment rate limits on notification dispatch. Validate notification recipients match the patient identity in the JWT before sending. Monitor third-party API usage and alert on anomalous volume spikes. Use message queues with backpressure controls.

data.name: SMS Provider
data.description: Third-party external SMS delivery service used by the Notification Service to send appointment reminder text messages to patients

data.name: Email Provider
data.description: Third-party external email delivery service used by the Notification Service to send appointment confirmation and reminder emails to patients

data.name: Portal Access (HTTPS)
data.description: Patient accesses portal login page and submits credentials over HTTPS from the public internet

data.name: Forward Request
data.description: Patient Portal forwards authenticated patient requests to the API Gateway for routing to backend services

data.name: Token Validation Request
data.description: API Gateway forwards JWT to the Identity Service for signature and claims verification before routing the request

data.name: JWT Validation Result
data.description: Identity Service returns validated claims and authorisation result to the API Gateway

data.name: Medical Record Request
data.description: API Gateway routes authenticated patient medical record read request to the Medical Records Service

data.name: Patient Record Response
data.description: Medical Records Service returns requested patient health data to the API Gateway for forwarding to the portal

data.name: Appointment Request
data.description: API Gateway routes authenticated appointment booking or viewing request to the Appointment Service

data.name: Read Patient Data
data.description: Appointment Service retrieves relevant patient data from the Medical Records Service to support appointment scheduling

data.name: Trigger Reminder
data.description: Appointment Service sends appointment details to the Notification Service to dispatch reminder messages to the patient

data.name: SMS Reminder (HTTPS)
data.description: Notification Service sends appointment reminder SMS request to the third-party SMS provider via HTTPS API

data.name: Email Reminder (SMTP)
data.description: Notification Service sends appointment reminder email to the third-party email provider via SMTP or HTTPS API

data.name: Portal Response (HTTPS)
data.description: Patient Portal returns requested information or confirmation to the patient browser over HTTPS
